methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Fortem Technologies is a Series C-stage defense and security company that has raised $100M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 same-sector peers spanning Series B and Series D stages, supplemented by two public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private peers including Saronic, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $100M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yields $170M. Peer-median methodology references 10 defense & security peers at comparable or adjacent stages; the median valuation across this set is $1.4B, with a range of $500M to $9.2B. The peer distribution reflects material heterogeneity, with Series D outliers (Saronic at $9.2B, Chaos Industries at $4.5B) substantially above Series B medians (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI each at $1.4B–$1.5B).

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $144M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $772M. The wide dispersion reflects uncertainty in stage-to-stage progression velocity within the sector and the substantial valuation spread among early-stage defense tech peers. Key sensitivities include revenue traction relative to peer cohorts, customer concentration, and regulatory or contractual risk in defense verticals.
